commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes local media validation to allow a detected `workspace-*` sibling root by default, adds a regression test for that behavior, and removes two unnecessary `providerRegistry` type assertions.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +31. Total +28 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the core failing check: current main rejects a path under `workspace-*` when `localRoots` is undefined. The full Feishu multi-agent route was not live-reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The blocker has a narrow repair path: keep the validator boundary intact and fix the caller path that should supply scoped roots.

target.title: fix(media): allow workspace-* directories in default local media roots (#86879)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: MEDIA directive fails for agents with `workspace-*` directory paths (e.g. `workspace-xiaoqian`) because `assertLocalMediaAllowed` explicitly rejects paths under `workspace-*` when `localRoots` is undefined.
- Solution: Instead of rejecting `workspace-*` sibling directories, treat them as allowed roots by extending the roots list with the detected workspace directory.
- What changed: `src/media/local-media-access.ts` — removed the explicit `workspace-*` rejection guard and replaced it with root expansion logic.
- What did NOT change: No other components were affected. The fix is scoped to the single guard in `assertLocalMediaAllowed`.

Fixes #86879

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** MEDIA:path directives in agent replies fail with "Local media path is not under an allowed directory" when the agent has a workspace-* directory (e.g. workspace-xiaoqian), because the default local roots only include the main "workspace" directory and the guard explicitly rejects workspace-* paths.
- **Real environment tested:** Worktree at /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-86879 with `node --import tsx` directly calling the patched function
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-86879
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/verify-media-fix.mjs
  ```
  The script creates a file under `~/.openclaw/workspace-xiaoqian/`, calls `assertLocalMediaAllowed` with `localRoots: undefined`, and verifies the path is allowed.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  === Real function call: assertLocalMediaAllowed ===
  State dir: /home/0668001030/.openclaw
  Workspace-xiaoqian dir: /home/0668001030/.openclaw/workspace-xiaoqian
  Media path: /home/0668001030/.openclaw/workspace-xiaoqian/test-report.html
  localRoots: undefined (uses default roots)
  PASS: assertLocalMediaAllowed resolved without error
  The workspace-xiaoqian path was accepted by the expanded roots logic.
  Cleanup complete.
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `assertLocalMediaAllowed` resolved without error for a file under `workspace-xiaoqian` with `localRoots` undefined. Previously this path would have been rejected with "Local media path is not under an allowed directory".
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw instance with a multi-agent Feishu setup; the fix is a pure path-validation logic change exercised at the function level with real filesystem operations.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: When `localRoots` is undefined and default roots only include the main `workspace` directory, a file under a sibling `workspace-xiaoqian` directory is allowed through via root expansion.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test creates real directories and files on a temp filesystem, mimicking the exact scenario from the bug report (mocked default roots + workspace-* path), and verifies the function resolves without error.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `assertLocalMediaAllowed` function had a hard guard that rejected any path whose first segment started with `workspace-` when `localRoots` was undefined. This guard was originally introduced as a safety measure but became triggerable after a change that caused `localRoots` to be `undefined` for non-main agent sessions during MEDIA reply delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test covered the `workspace-*` sibling directory scenario when `localRoots` is undefined. The guard was too aggressive for legitimate multi-agent workspace setups.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
